texto: A storm was coming; you could feel it in the air, chill wind was cutting through the thickest coat and there was a faint wetness in the air that was touching the skin if turned towards the storm. Mark looked at the sky above the ocean. The sky was getting darker it no longer felt like midday, the storm moved the clock forward and it felt more like the beginnings of sunset. With lightening flashes every few minutes, the sky would light up all of a sudden. It was magnificent, breathtaking and even life taking.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Austin V. Songer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “A storm was coming; you could feel it in the air, chill wind was cutting through the thickest coa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, lost-love, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao-noturna.

texto: All that matters is this moment and what you make of it, because the past is history and the future is just full of wishes that you can’t foresee.
explicacao: Citação de Austin V. Songer sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “All that matters is this moment and what you make of it, because the past is history and the futu…” articula reflexao, future, matters em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
